title: thelypteris sancta var. portoricensis
intro: Thelypteris sancta var. portoricensis, also known as the Puerto Rican shield fern, is a rare and endemic species of fern found in Puerto Rico. It belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae and is characterized by its unique shield-shaped fronds. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are said to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
